name: GPT-3.5 Supplemented Small Student Text Response Sets
statement: Cochran and colleagues used GPT-3.5 to generate artificial student responses from small student text datasets, then used the augmented data to train multilingual BERT classifiers.
population_or_context: French opinion responses and sixth-grade English rainwater-runoff responses.
quantitative_note: The study reports that augmented models outperformed unaugmented BERT in all seven concepts and that GPT-3.5 generation achieved the maximum performance in four of seven cases.
evaluation_method: Micro-F1 comparison among majority-label baseline, unaugmented BERT, self-augmentation, and GPT-3.5 augmentation.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: Small educational text datasets with label imbalance.
synthetic_data_research_role: Generated additional training examples to supplement small sets and test whether artificial responses outperform simple replication.
reported_metrics: Micro-F1; augmentation peak and degradation levels.
limitations: For very low-entropy datasets, majority-label prediction still outperformed trained classifiers.
Performance degraded after very high augmentation levels.
